formatting_policy.default: plain_text unless field contract says otherwise
formatting_policy.inline_markdown: Supports bold, italic, inline code, and inline math using $...$.
formatting_policy.block_markdown: Supports paragraphs, lists, bold, italic, and math using $...$ or $$...$$.
formatting_policy.latex_raw: Raw LaTeX only. Do not wrap in $...$ unless field contract says markdown math.
formatting_policy.plain_phrase_list: Plain phrases, no markdown. Usually used for matching and highlighting.
formatting_policy.positioned_callouts: Labels positioned using x/y percentages from 0 to 100.

1.name: Orient
1.description: Set direction, context, purpose, or closure.

2.name: Build Knowledge
2.description: Define, compare, organize, or anchor key knowledge.

3.name: Model
3.description: Show a method, example, or process before independent work.

4.name: Practice and Check
4.description: Let students answer, practise, retrieve, reflect, or show thinking.

5.name: Address Mistakes
5.description: Warn against misconceptions or correct common errors.

6.name: Visualize
6.description: Make spatial, relational, chronological, or visual structure visible.

7.name: Interact
7.description: Let learners manipulate, observe, or explore a concept interactively.

section-header.role: Opens a section with title, subject, objective, and level indicators

section_number.description: Optional numeric or ordinal section label.

grade_band.description: Audience level band for the section.

level_pills.renderBehavior: Structured items with label and warm/medium/cold variant.

component_constraints: Keep title concise; subtitle optional for tonal context.

hook-hero.role: Creates felt need before explanation arrives

body.renderBehavior: Usually inline markdown. When type is quote, treat as plain quote text without markdown emphasis unless explicitly needed.

anchor.description: Short anchor tying the hook to the lesson concept.

type.description: Hook presentation mode (prose, quote, question, data-point).
constraints: When type is question, provide question_options.
When type is data-point, provide data_point.

question_options.description: Optional multiple choice stems for question hooks.

data_point.description: Structured numeric or textual evidence for data-point hooks.
data_point.renderBehavior: Rendered as a compact fact card when present.

image.description: Optional inline illustration with url and alt text.
image.renderBehavior: Rendered as an inline image when present.

constraints: SVG must be trusted or sanitized.

component_constraints: Anchor should be short and specific to the section concept.
Match optional payloads to the selected hook type.

print_behavior.fallback: Pull quote block with left border

explanation-block.role: Sustained prose that builds a mental model

body.description: Main explanation text for the section.
body.renderBehavior: Rendered as block markdown. Supports markdown and LaTeX math.

emphasis.description: Phrases highlighted inside the explanation body.
emphasis.renderBehavior: Lectio highlights matching phrases in the body.
constraints: Use plain phrases.
Do not use markdown in emphasis items.
Each phrase should appear in body.
Prefer key terms or short phrases, not full sentences.

callouts.description: Optional sideline notes with a tone tag and text.
callouts.renderBehavior: Displayed as optional notes associated with the explanation block.

component_constraints: Put the primary teaching narrative in body.
Use emphasis for concepts that should visually pop when they recur in body.

prerequisite-strip.role: Lists assumed knowledge with optional refresher pop-ups
component_constraints: List only prerequisites the section truly depends on.

what-next-bridge.role: Connects the section forward to what the concept enables

body.description: Bridge narrative from this section to upcoming work.

next.description: Short label for what comes next.

preview.description: Optional preview blurb of the next chunk.

prerequisites.description: Optional short prerequisites the learner should recall.

component_constraints: Keep next concrete and short.

interview-anchor.role: Makes knowledge speakable - rehearse explaining the concept
component_constraints: Keep audience concrete and short.

callout-block.role: Standalone highlighted callout ΓÇö tip, warning, info, or exam note

variant.description: Callout tone (info, tip, warning, exam-tip, remember).

heading.description: Optional short heading above the body.

body.renderBehavior: Block markdown with support for emphasis and short lists.

component_constraints: Use headings sparingly; variant already carries tone.

summary-block.role: Lists what this section covered ΓÇö key takeaways as bullets
component_constraints: Keep items tight and parallel in structure where possible.

section-divider.role: Named visual break between parts within a section
component_constraints: Use a concise transition label, not a second heading.

definition-card.role: Anchors a formal term with formal and plain versions

term.description: The defined term as a heading.

constraints: Use raw LaTeX for heavy notation.
If content is mostly prose, prefer inline_markdown stored elsewhere instead.

examples.renderBehavior: Rendered as plain example lines (no extra quotes added by contract).

component_constraints: plain should read approachable; formal should be more precise.
Do not wrap examples in quotation marks if the renderer already decorates them.

definition-family.role: Groups related terms that belong together conceptually

definitions.renderBehavior: Each entry uses the same field semantics as definition-card.

component_constraints: Keep related definitions grouped by a single conceptual family.

glossary-rail.role: Vocabulary visible in peripheral field, updates by section

terms[].used_in.description: Optional note about where the word appears in the lesson.

component_constraints: Keep definitions short and classroom-ready for the rail layout.

terms.definition: A Greek letter commonly used as a variable.

print_behavior.fallback: Inline vocabulary strip at section end

insight-strip.role: Side-by-side comparison of 2-3 related values or concepts

cells[].highlight.description: Optional emphasis flag for the cell.

component_constraints: Keep each cell to a single insight; avoid long paragraphs per cell.

key-fact.role: Visually prominent stat, formula, or date that anchors the section

fact.renderBehavior: Inline markdown with optional emphasis and math.

component_constraints: Keep the fact line short and exam-ready.

comparison-grid.role: Holds multiple concepts in view so distinctions become structural

columns[].id.renderBehavior: Plain text key used for row mapping.
constraints: Keep ids stable and short.

rows[].criterion.description: Row label for the compared criterion.

rows[].values.description: Per-column cell values in column order.
rows[].values.renderBehavior: Plain text entries aligned to columns by index.

rows[].takeaway.description: Optional short synthesis for the row.

apply_prompt.description: Optional apply/synthesis prompt after the grid.

component_constraints: Each row.values array length must match the number of columns.
Use stable column ids when referencing columns in downstream tooling.

worked-example-card.role: Shows reasoning in action step by step, each step justified

title.description: Short title for the worked example.
title.renderBehavior: Rendered as a readable heading line.

setup.renderBehavior: Inline markdown with optional emphasis and inline math.

steps[].label.description: Short label for each reasoning step.

steps[].content.renderBehavior: Block markdown with support for lists and math.

steps[].note.description: Optional side note for a step.

steps[].formula.description: Standalone equation material for the step.
constraints: Use raw LaTeX.
Do not wrap in delimiters unless the renderer applies math mode.

answer.description: Short canonical answer line when provided.

diagram.description: Optional diagram attached to the worked example.
diagram.renderBehavior: Rendered with the shared diagram surface when present.

component_constraints: Each step should represent one clear reasoning move.
Keep formulas as raw LaTeX in formula fields.

process-steps.role: A repeatable procedure where order is non-negotiable

constraints: Numbers should be sequential.

steps[].action.description: Short verb-led action for the step.

steps[].input.description: Short input label for the step.

steps[].output.description: Short output label for the step.

component_constraints: Action should be short and verb-first.
detail expands the action; input/output are labels, not paragraphs.

print_behavior.fallback: All steps visible, checkbox squares for print

practice-stack.role: Problems at calibrated difficulty with progressive hints and optional solutions

problems[].question.description: The problem statement shown to the learner.

problems[].hints[].text.description: Progressive hint text for a given hint level.
problems[].hints[].text.renderBehavior: Rendered as block markdown inside the hint stack.

constraints: Levels should increase in strength.
Use 1 (light), 2 (medium), or 3 (strong).

problems[].solution.approach.description: High-level solution approach when a solution is provided.

problems[].diagram.description: Optional instructional diagram payload matching DiagramContent.
problems[].diagram.renderBehavior: Rendered using the shared diagram renderer when present.

problems[].difficulty.description: Declared difficulty band for the problem.

component_constraints: Order hints from light support to stronger support.
Problem difficulty must use the supported enum values.

print_behavior.fallback: All visible, write-in lines rendered; inline answers hidden by default

quiz-check.role: Quick concept check with immediate feedback mid-section

options[].text.description: Visible option text shown to the learner.

options[].correct.description: Whether this option is the correct answer.

feedback_correct.description: Short feedback when the learner is correct.

feedback_incorrect.description: Short feedback when the learner is incorrect.

quiz_type.description: Optional quiz mode discriminator (multiple-choice vs true-false).

component_constraints: Every option should include an explanation.
Prefer exactly one correct option unless the quiz explicitly supports otherwise.
Do not prefix option text with A/B/C labels in the string; the UI numbers options.

print_behavior.fallback: Question and options shown, correct answer marked

reflection-prompt.role: Pauses forward motion and turns attention inward

type.description: Reflection mode (open, pair-share, sentence-stem, timed, etc.).
constraints: When type is sentence-stem, fill sentence_stem.
When type is timed, set time_minutes.
When type is pair-share, set pair_instruction.

space.description: Reserved vertical space for written responses when applicable.
space.renderBehavior: Controls print line affordance, not screen layout.

sentence_stem.description: Starter clause when using sentence-stem mode.

component_constraints: Choose optional fields that match the reflection type.

student-textbox.role: Simple write-in box for student responses ΓÇö no framing beyond a prompt

lines.description: Optional number of ruled lines for print layouts.
lines.renderBehavior: Controls ruled line count, not screen layout height.

label.description: Optional accessible label for the input.

component_constraints: Keep prompts concrete and tied to a single task.

short-answer.role: Open question with write-in space and optional mark scheme
short-answer.cognitive_job: Recall and explain in own words

marks.renderBehavior: Numeric marks shown to learners when provided.

lines.description: Optional ruled line count for print.

mark_scheme.description: Optional marking guidance for teachers or auto-graders.

component_constraints: Keep mark_scheme aligned to the stated marks when both exist.

print_behavior.fallback: Question with lined answer space, mark allocation shown

fill-in-blank.role: Cloze passage with student-completed blanks ΓÇö tests recall not recognition

segments[].is_blank.description: Whether this segment is rendered as a blank.
segments[].is_blank.renderBehavior: Controls blank UI for the segment.

segments[].answer.description: Answer text when is_blank is true.
constraints: Required whenever is_blank is true.

word_bank.renderBehavior: Plain text entries; should cover blank answers when provided.

component_constraints: Alternate plain text segments with blanks as needed.
Every blank segment should have an answer.
If word_bank exists, include answers for all blanks.

print_behavior.fallback: Passage with underlined blanks, word bank box below

pitfall-alert.role: Names a specific misconception before the learner makes it

examples.renderBehavior: Inline markdown entries when multiple examples are needed.

component_constraints: Correction should directly address the misconception line.
Prefer examples[] when you need more than one short illustration.

diagram-block.role: Makes spatial or relational structure visible

image_url.renderBehavior: Rendered as a responsive when present. Callout overlays use the same percentage coordinates as SVG paths.

svg_content.renderBehavior: Sanitized SVG inserted when image_url is absent.
constraints: SVG must be safe/trusted content before sanitization.

caption.description: Figure caption displayed under the diagram.

zoom_label.description: Short label used in zoom/detail affordances.

figure_number.renderBehavior: Displayed as a figure chip when provided.

callouts.description: Labeled points on the diagram with percentage coordinates.
callouts.renderBehavior: Rendered as positioned overlays on raster or SVG output. x/y are percentages from 0 to 100 relative to the diagram viewport.
constraints: Keep labels short.
Explanations should clarify the labeled region.
Compatible with image-based and SVG-based diagrams.

component_constraints: Provide either image_url or svg_content for a visible diagram.
Keep callout coordinates inside 0–100 for both raster and vector diagrams.

examples.caption: A labelled diagram for the lesson.
examples.alt_text: Diagram showing key parts referenced in the prose.

diagram-compare.role: Before and after comparison with a drag slider

before_svg.description: Optional SVG markup for the before panel.

after_svg.description: Optional SVG markup for the after panel.

before_image_url.description: Optional raster image URL for the before panel.

after_image_url.description: Optional raster image URL for the after panel.

before_label.description: Short label for the before column.

after_label.description: Short label for the after column.

before_details.description: Optional bullet strings elaborating the before state.

after_details.description: Optional bullet strings elaborating the after state.

caption.description: Shared caption for the compare figure.

component_constraints: Provide either SVG or image URLs for each side you want to show.
Keep column labels short; they appear in compact headers.

print_behavior.fallback: Both diagrams shown side by side

diagram-series.role: A progression of diagrams that tells a sequence

title.description: Title for the multi-step diagram series.

diagrams[].step_label.description: Label for the step position in the series.

diagrams[].caption.description: Caption for the individual step diagram.

diagrams[].svg_content.description: Optional SVG markup for the step.

diagrams[].image_url.description: Optional raster image for the step.

component_constraints: Each step should include at least one visual source (SVG or image).
Keep step labels parallel in tone and length.

print_behavior.fallback: All diagrams in sequence with step labels

timeline-block.role: Turns chronology into a readable instructional spine
timeline-block.cognitive_job: Follow cause and sequence over time

events[].id.description: Stable identifier for the event row.

events[].tags.description: Optional short tag strings for filtering or emphasis.

component_constraints: Events should usually appear in chronological order.
Each event should have a stable id.

simulation-block.role: Manipulate a variable and discover the concept through observation

spec.type.description: Discriminator string for the embedded interaction.
spec.type.renderBehavior: Plain text token consumed by the interaction host.

spec.goal.description: Human-readable learning goal for the interaction.

spec.anchor_content.description: Structured anchor parameters for the interaction.
spec.anchor_content.renderBehavior: Arbitrary JSON object interpreted by the interaction renderer.

spec.context.description: Visual context for the interaction (theme, colors, learner level).
spec.context.renderBehavior: Passed to the interaction shell for styling and layout.

spec.dimensions.description: Width/height hints for the iframe surface.

spec.print_translation.description: How the interaction degrades in print (static snapshot, hide, etc.).

html_content.description: Optional HTML bundle for iframe embedding.
html_content.renderBehavior: Trusted HTML inserted into the sandboxed iframe host.

fallback_diagram.description: Optional DiagramContent used when iframe content is unavailable.

explanation.description: Optional instructor-facing explanation of the interaction.

component_constraints: Provide either html_content or a meaningful fallback_diagram for offline contexts.
Keep spec.goal aligned with what the iframe interaction practices.

spec.goal: Explore the slider effect on the graph.

image-block.reason: Editor-attached image content, not a generation-facing section block.

video-embed.reason: Editor-attached video content, not a generation-facing section block.

glossary-inline.reason: Inline helper content, not a standalone section block.
